title: fix bug check nonexist authfile
body:
For podman command(except login), if authfile does not exist returns error.

close #4328 
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
/approve
Change LGTM, but CI is broken

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *QiWang19* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Can't we just do the shared.GetAuthFile() in the options handling, so we check if the environment variable is set and then we don't need to change all this code?

this will fail if I don't have default  /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json, should we consider this situation that ? I think CI fails for this reason
```
podman search alpine
ERRO[0000] error checking authfile path /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json: stat /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json: no such file or directory 

```

I would like to remove the /run/user... stuff from defaults and allow containers/image to handle it, especially if we move to Keyrings as the default, Then we can rely on C/I to specify the default localtion.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4310) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

LGTM
Thanks for sticking at it on this PR @QiWang19 !
and green test buttons if someone wants to LGTM it and merge.

> and green test buttons if someone wants to LGTM it and merge.

merged this PR after buildah PR https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1967 got merged so I can vendor in?
@QiWang19 I'm a bit confused by your last comment.  Do you want to merge this after containers/buildah#1967 gets merged?  Then you want to vendor Buildah into Podman once that's in?
@TomSweeneyRedHat yes https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4337#discussion_r342794897 if I should add this utility function. That function will be used both in buildah and podman.
